---
title: "ss_taylor"
format: revealjs
---



```{r}
#| echo: false

im_pom <- discovr::eddiefy |> 
  filter(album_name == "Piece of Mind") |> 
  mutate(track_number = 1:9)
ts_1989 <- read_rds("data/taylorfy.RDS") |> 
  filter(album_name == "1989") |> 
  mutate(track_number = 1:13)
```

## How do we tell if a model is a good fit?

- Let's look at a simple model: the mean
- How do we tell if it's a good fit?
- With some help from Taylor Swift
  - The album **1989**

- `r spotify()` produce measures of song content
  - Energy
  - Valence
  - Danceability

>   "Energy is a measure from 0.0 to 1.0 and represents a perceptual measure of intensity and activity." (Spotify, API)

* The `spotifyr` package scrapes this data!

::: notes
Taylor swift hit 'shake it off' from the album 1989. We're interested in the extent to which the mean (our model) energy value reflects the entire album.
:::

## Is the average energy score a good fit?

::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 6

ts_mean <- mean(ts_1989$energy)
ts_ss <- sum((ts_1989$energy - ts_mean)^2)
im_mean <-mean(im_pom$energy)
im_ss <- sum((im_pom$energy - im_mean)^2)


ss_1989 <- ggplot(ts_1989, aes(track_number, energy)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = mean(ts_mean), colour = blue) +
  labs(x = "Track number", y = "Energy (0-1)") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits=c(0, 13), breaks=0:13) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18) + 
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) +
  annotate("text", x = 12, y = 0.60, label = sprintf("%.2f", ts_mean), size = 7, colour = blue)

ss_1989
```

:::

![](media/ts_1989.png){.absolute left=100 bottom=210 width="200"}

::: notes
The mean energy level is 0.7 (quite high), but does this reflect every song? If you want a high energy album, is that what you're getting? How good is the mean as an indicator of the album's energy levels?

Heavy metal gets a bad rap for 'backward messages' ... Judas Priest etc. But no-one ever does the control comparison of reversing other songs. I did this to Taylor Swift's 'shake it up' with surprising results.
:::

## Is the average energy score a good fit?

:::: columns 
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r, echo = F, fig.width = 6, fig.height = 6}
ss_1989 +
    geom_point(colour = brown, size = 4)
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{=html}
<audio controls>
  <source src="media/taylor_swift_satan.mp3" type="audio/mpeg">
  <source src="media/taylor_swift_satan.ogg" type="audio/ogg"/>
</audio>
```
:::
:::

![](media/ts_1989.png){.absolute left=100 bottom=210 width="200"}

::: notes
I think it says '"Worship Satan for he is your dark lord and master, Sacrifice goats to Beelzibub ... you know you want to". See what you think.
:::

## Is the average energy score a good fit?

:::: columns 
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 6

ss_1989 +
    geom_point(colour = brown, size = 4)
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{=html}
<audio controls>
  <source src="media/taylor_swift_satan.mp3" type="audio/mpeg">
  <source src="media/taylor_swift_satan.ogg" type="audio/ogg"/>
</audio>
```

"Worship Satan for he is your dark lord and master,

Sacrifice goats to Beelzibub ... 

you know you want to"

:::
:::

![](media/ts_1989.png){.absolute left=100 bottom=210 width="200"}

## Is the average energy score a good fit?


::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 6

ss_1989 +
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = ts_mean, ymax = energy), size = 1, colour = blue, linetype = "longdash") +
  geom_point(colour = brown, size = 4) +
  annotate("text", x = 6.5, y = 1, label = paste("SS =", sprintf("%.2f", ts_ss)), size = 10, colour = blue)
```
:::

![](media/ts_1989.png){.absolute left=100 bottom=210 width="200"}

::: notes
So we can quantify 'fit' using sums of squares. In this very simple case the total error in prediction from using the mean is 0.26, but what does this mean? Ideally we need a frame of reference. Let's look at the same data for the greatest album ever written Piece of Mind by Iron Maiden.
:::

## {background-video="media/flight_of_icarus_vancouver.mp4" background-size="cover"}


## Is the average energy score a good fit?

:::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 6

ss_1989 +
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = ts_mean, ymax = energy), size = 1, colour = blue, linetype = "longdash") +
  geom_point(colour = brown, size = 4) +
  annotate("text", x = 6.5, y = 1, label = paste("SS =", sprintf("%.2f", ts_ss)), size = 10, colour = blue)
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 6

ss_pom <- ggplot(im_pom, aes(track_number, energy)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = mean(im_mean), colour = blue) +
  labs(x = "Track number", y = "Energy (0-1)") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits=c(0, 9), breaks=0:9) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18) + 
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) +
  annotate("text", x = 8, y = 0.75, label = sprintf("%.2f", im_mean), size = 7, colour = blue)

ss_pom
```
:::
:::

![](media/ts_1989.png){.absolute left=100 bottom=210 width="200"}
![](media/piece_of_mind.jpg){.absolute left=635 bottom=210 width="200"}

::: notes
On average the energy rating of this album is higher than Taylor Swift's. We don't care about this pers e, we're interested in the extent to which this score reflects the entire album.
:::

## Is the average energy score a good fit?

:::: columns 
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 6

ss_1989 +
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = ts_mean, ymax = energy), size = 1, colour = blue, linetype = "longdash") +
  geom_point(colour = brown, size = 4) +
  annotate("text", x = 6.5, y = 1, label = paste("SS =", sprintf("%.2f", ts_ss)), size = 10, colour = blue)
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 6

ss_pom +
    geom_point(colour = brown, size = 4)
```
:::
:::

![](media/ts_1989.png){.absolute left=100 bottom=210 width="200"}
![](media/piece_of_mind.jpg){.absolute left=635 bottom=210 width="200"}

::: notes
Here's the energy values for each of the 9 tracks
:::

## Is the average energy score a good fit?

:::: columns 
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 6

ss_1989 +
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = ts_mean, ymax = energy), size = 1, colour = blue, linetype = "longdash") +
  geom_point(colour = brown, size = 4) +
  annotate("text", x = 6.5, y = 1, label = paste("SS =", sprintf("%.2f", ts_ss)), size = 10, colour = blue)
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 6

ss_pom +
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = im_mean, ymax = energy), size = 1, colour = blue, linetype = "longdash") +
  geom_point(colour = brown, size = 4) +
  annotate("text", x = 4.5, y = 1, label = paste("SS =", sprintf("%.2f", im_ss)), size = 10, colour = blue)
```

:::
:::

![](media/ts_1989.png){.absolute left=100 bottom=210 width="200"}
![](media/piece_of_mind.jpg){.absolute left=635 bottom=210 width="200"}

::: notes
We can go through the same process and find SS = 0.02. The sum of squares is much lower than for Taylor Swift. However, for TS the SS is based on 13 tracks and for IM it's based on 9, so TS will be larger because more scores were added.

If we want to compare SSs we have to factor in the number of scores.
:::

## Comparing sums of squares

- Sums of squares represent **total** error
- Because sums of squares are totals we can compare them only when they are based on the same number of scores.
- Alternatively, we factor in the number of scores
- We can get the **average** error by divide by a function of the number of scores
  - The degrees of freedom (the number of independent pieces of information)
  - The number of scores minus the number of parameters
  - $\text{df} = N-p$
  

::: center-h
::: txt_mulberry
::: txt_l
$$
\begin{aligned}
\text{MS} &= \frac{\text{SS}}{N-p} \\
&= \frac{\text{SS}}{N-1}
\end{aligned}
$$
:::
:::
:::

## Is the average energy score a good fit?

:::: columns 
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 6

ts_var <- var(ts_1989$energy)
im_var <- var(im_pom$energy)

ss_1989 +
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = ts_mean, ymax = energy), size = 1, colour = blue, linetype = "longdash") +
  geom_point(colour = brown, size = 4) +
  annotate("text", x = 6.5, y = 1, label = paste("MS =", sprintf("%.3f", ts_var)), size = 10, colour = mulberry)
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 6

ss_pom +
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = im_mean, ymax = energy), size = 1, colour = blue, linetype = "longdash") +
  geom_point(colour = brown, size = 4) +
  annotate("text", x = 4.5, y = 1, label = paste("MS =", sprintf("%.3f", im_var)), size = 10, colour = mulberry)
```
:::
:::

![](media/ts_1989.png){.absolute left=100 bottom=210 width="200"}
![](media/piece_of_mind.jpg){.absolute left=635 bottom=210 width="200"}


::: notes
We can compare the MS. Shows that the mean energy levels are a better fit for the iron maiden data ... there is less error in prediction, it better represents the entire album.
:::
